ogTitle: Shalvey vs Shanes Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Shalvey and Shanes Park.
heading: Shalvey vs Shanes Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shalvey, New South Wales and Shanes Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shanes Park has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Shalvey (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shalvey or Shanes Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shalvey has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Shanes Park scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shalvey scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Shanes Park scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shalvey or Shanes Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shanes Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
